content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 27, 1992

Honorable Gaddi H. Vasquez
County Supervisor, Third District
Robert E. Thomas Hall of Administration
10 Civic Center Plaza
Santa Ana, CA 92701-4061

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-171

Dear Supervisor Vasquez:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice regarding your responsibilities under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") that regulate the receipt of gifts and the provisions of the Act that govern the permissible use of campaign funds.

QUESTIONS

1. May you accept a gift of transportation and accommodations from the British Government to attend a program in the United Kingdom intended to acquaint American public officials with the economic, political, and social systems of the United Kingdom and promote trade between Orange County and the United Kingdom?

2. May you use campaign funds to pay for transportation and accommodations for your spouse to attend the United Kingdom program?

3. May you use campaign funds to pay for travel and accommodations for you, your spouse and dependent child to visit EuroDisney in Paris, France as part of a program organized by the Disney Company to acquaint Orange County public officials with the

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-171
Page 2


technology and improvements they intend to bring to Disneyland in
Orange County?

CONCLUSIONS

     1.  Reimbursement for actual travel expenses and reasonable
subsistence in connection with your attendance at any public or
private conference, convention, meeting, social event, meal, or
like gathering is not subject to the $1,000 gift/honoraria limit
applicable to local elected officials.  However, you will be
required to disclose any source of gifts of $50 or more in value.
In addition, you are prohibited from making, participating in
making or in any way attempting to use your official position to
influence a governmental decision which will have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on any donor of, or any
intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
$250 or more in value provided to, received by, or promised to you
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

     2.  Campaign funds may be used for your spouse to accompany
you on the United Kingdom trip if you would be able to use
campaign funds to make the trip yourself.  If the trip to the
United Kingdom to enhance trade is related to your status as an
officeholder, you may use campaign funds for the trip.

     3.  If the trip to tour EuroDisney is related to a
governmental decision you will be making, you may use campaign
funds for ordinary and necessary expenses associated with the
travel and accommodations consistent with the Internal Revenue
Service standards.  In addition, if you are permitted to use
campaign funds to travel to tour EuroDisney, you may use campaign
funds for your spouse and dependent child when they are traveling
to the same destination in order to accompany you.

FACTS

     You are the Chairman of the Orange County Board of
Supervisors.  You have been invited to participate in two foreign
travel programs.

     The first program will be fully funded by the British
Government and involves a program in the United Kingdom intended
to acquaint American public officials with the economic,
political, and social systems of the United Kingdom and promote
trade between Orange County and the United Kingdom.  The itinerary
will include visits with public officials, speeches, and visits to
public institutions and facilities.  However, the British
government will not pay for your spouse's expenses.  You have
asked whether you may use campaign funds to pay for your spouse to
accompany you.

     The second program was organized by the Disney Company for
Orange County officials.  The Disney Company is planning on a

Our File No. A-92-171
Page 3


multi-billion dollar expansion of Disneyland in Orange County.
The Disney Company has organized a trip to view a sister park in
Paris, France called EuroDisney which features much of the
technology and improvements that the company plans to bring to
Disneyland in Orange County. As part of the trip you will meet
Disney executives, designers and local government leaders. You
have asked whether you may use campaign funds to pay for your
spouse and 12-year old child to accompany you.

ANALYSIS

I.  The United Kingdom Trip

    A.  Gifts From a Foreign Government

You have asked whether you may accept free air transportation
and accommodations from the British Government as part of a
program intended to acquaint American public officials with the
economic, political and social systems in the United Kingdom.

Local elected officers are subject to a limit on gifts and
honoraria received from a single source in a calendar year.
Section 89501 provides:

     No local elected officeholder shall accept any
     honoraria for any speech given, article published,
     or attendance at any public or private conference,
     convention, meeting, social event, meal, or like
     gathering, or any gifts, from any single source,
     which is in excess of one thousand dollars
     ($1,000), in any calendar year, except
     reimbursement for actual travel expenses and
     reasonable subsistence in connection therewith.

However, the statute specifically excludes from the $1,000
limit reimbursement for actual travel expenses and reasonable
subsistence in connection with a speech given, article published,
or attendance at any public or private conference, convention,
meeting, social event, meal, or like gathering.  Thus, the
reimbursement for your travel expenses by the British Government
would not be considered for purposes of the $1,000 gift limit in
Section 89501.

Please note, however, absent some other express exception in
the Act, all public officials must disclose the name and address
of each source of gifts of $50 or more in value, the amount and
the date on which the gift was received, and a general description
of the business activity of the donor.  (Section 87207.)
Moreover, you are prohibited from making, participating in making
or in any way attempting to use your official position to
influence a government decision which will have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on any donor of, or any
intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating

Our File No. A-92-171
Page 4


$250 or more in value provided to, received by, or promised to you
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.
(Sections 87100, et seq.)

B. Use of Campaign Funds

You have also asked if you may use campaign funds to pay for
transportation and accommodations in connection with the United
Kingdom program for your spouse. The general rule of the
"personal use" provisions of the Act which regulate the
expenditure of campaign funds is that any expenditure of campaign
funds must be, at a minimum, reasonably related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose. However, where an
expenditure confers a substantial personal benefit on the
candidate or elected officer, the expenditure must be directly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.2
(Section 89512.)

Moreover, the personal use law sets forth specific standards
concerning specific types of expenditures. Section 89513(a)(1),
which deals with travel, provides:

(a) Campaign funds shall not be used to pay
or reimburse the candidate, the elected officer, or
any individual or individuals with authority to
approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by a
committee, or employees or staff of the committee
or the elected officer's governmental agency for
travel expenses and necessary accommodations except
when these expenditures are directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

(1) For the purposes of this section,
payments or reimbursements for travel and
necessary accommodations shall be considered
as directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose if the
payments would meet standards similar to the
standards of the Internal Revenue Service
pursuant to Sections 162 and 274 of the
Internal Revenue Code for deductions of travel
expenses under the federal income tax law.

(2) For the purposes of this section,
payments or reimbursement for travel by the
household of a candidate or elected officer
when travelling to the same destination in
order to accompany the candidate or elected
officer shall be considered for the same

2 A substantial personal benefit is defined as a direct personal
benefit to the candidate or elected officer of $100 or more.
(Section 89511(b)(2).)

Our File No. A-92-171
Page 5


purpose as the candidate's or elected
officer's travel.

Thus, the test set forth in Section 89513(a)(2) is whether
there is a direct relationship between a governmental purpose and
the trip you wish to take.³ Absent such a relationship, Section
89513(a)(1) precludes the use of campaign funds for travel.
Moreover, according to Section 89513(a)(2), to the extent that it
is permissible for you to use campaign funds for your travel and
accommodations, you may use campaign funds for your spouse when
she is traveling to the same destination in order to accompany
you. Your spouse is deemed to be traveling for the same purpose
as you are.⁴

Section 89513(a)(2) provides that payments or reimbursements
for travel and necessary accommodations shall be considered as
directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose if the payments would meet standards similar to the
standards of the Internal Revenue Service pursuant to Sections 162
and 274 of the Internal Revenue Code for deductions of travel
expenses under the federal income tax law.

If the enhancement of trade with the United Kingdom may be
related to your status as an officeholder, campaign funds may be
used for ordinary and necessary expenses associated with the
travel and accommodations consistent with the Internal Revenue
Service standards for the deduction of "business expenses" in
connection with your position.

Internal Revenue Code Section 162(a)(2) sets forth general
rules which permit the deduction of all ordinary and necessary
traveling expenses while away from home in the pursuit of a trade
or business. Pursuant to this section, campaign funds may be used
for "business portions" of the trip. To the extent that any day
is not a business day (such as days on which there are no meetings
or formal activities), campaign funds may not be used.⁵


_________________________
³ The Commission is presently developing regulations defining
when travel is directly related to a political, legislative or
governmental purpose.

⁴ The Act would apply the same "shared purpose" standard for your
household, which includes your spouse, dependent children, and
parents, if your parents reside with you. (Section 89511(b)(3).)

⁵ A travel day is considered a business day if followed
immediately by a day that is devoted, at least in part, to a
business purpose. Moreover, where a weekend falls between
business days, the weekend will also be counted as business time.

Our File No. A-92-171
Page 6


Moreover, personal activities not related to a governmental
purpose may not be paid for with campaign funds, even if those
activities, such as a sight-seeing, occur on a business day. The
amount of campaign funds that can be utilized for transportation
costs can be determined by looking at the ratio of business-
related days to the total number of days in the United Kingdom.

II. EuroDisney Trip

You have also asked whether you may use campaign funds to pay
for transportation to and accommodations in Paris, France to tour
EuroDisney as part of a delegation from Orange County arranged by
the Disney Company. The tour is intended to acquaint you with the
technology that has been proposed for use at Disneyland in Orange
County. As described above, you may do so only if there is a
direct relationship between a governmental purpose and the trip to
Paris.

If the trip to tour EuroDisney is related to the governmental
decision you will make with respect to the park expansion, you may
use campaign funds to pay for the ordinary and necessary expenses
associated with the trip, including travel and accommodations with
the caveats set forth above. You have also asked whether you may
use campaign funds to pay for transportation to and accommodations
of your spouse and dependent child so that they may accompany you
to the program in Paris. As stated above, if you are permitted to
use campaign funds to travel to tour EuroDisney, you may use
campaign funds for your spouse and dependent child when they are
traveling to the same destination in order to accompany you.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.7

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JMW:aa


7 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
